blanked: The chess ______ defeated adult grandmasters when he was only nine years old.

definition: skilled and competent at doing something

definition: to increase rapidly in number or spread widely

definition: ideas spread to sway what people think, often in a one-sided way

definition: a document giving facts about a school, company, or investment

definition: the official rules for how things should be done
blanked: Safety ______ requires all visitors to sign in at the front desk before entering the building.

definition: a large section of a country with its own local government
blanked: Canada is divided into ten ______, each with its own premier and legislature.

definition: nearness in distance or time

definition: showing careful judgment to avoid risks

definition: to officially approve and make something valid

definition: a break from work or school, or a hidden alcove

definition: a vote in which all citizens decide on a single important question

blanked: The company will ______ you for any supplies you buy for the school project.

definition: a time of great art and learning, around the 1400s to 1600s
blanked: During the ______, artists like Leonardo da Vinci created masterpieces that still amaze people today.

definition: to fill something up again after it has been used

definition: a place where things are stored or can be found

definition: to hold back or prevent the expression of feelings or actions
blanked: She tried to ______ her laughter during the serious meeting, but a giggle escaped.

definition: to scold or blame someone for doing something wrong

definition: to refuse to accept or be associated with something

definition: needed for a certain purpose

definition: a large natural or man-made lake used for storing water
blanked: The ______ holds enough water to supply the entire city for six months.

definition: remaining after the main part has been removed or used

definition: able to recover quickly from problems; tough and able to bend without breaking
blanked: Bamboo is incredibly ______—it bends in strong winds but almost never breaks.

definition: the quality of being deep and full, or of evoking strong feelings

definition: payment or action to make up for loss or damage
blanked: The boy had to pay ______ for the neighbor's window he broke with his baseball.

definition: to pull back or take back something previously said

definition: looking back on past events with the knowledge one has now
blanked: In ______, she realized that studying every night had been the key to her success.

definition: the art of using language well to persuade people
blanked: The candidate's strong ______ convinced many undecided voters to support her.

definition: very thorough, having many rules, and demanding

definition: a list of names showing who is part of a team or group
blanked: The coach posted the team ______ on the bulletin board, so players could check if they made the cut.

definition: a set of guidelines used for grading work

definition: to break open or burst suddenly

definition: clean and safe from germs and a sickness
blanked: Restaurant kitchens must meet strict ______ standards, so customers stay healthy.

definition: a metal frame workers stand on to reach high parts of a building
blanked: Workers stood on the ______ to paint the outside of the three-story building.

definition: the enforced separation of different groups of people

definition: relating to or caused by earthquakes

definition: a small class or meeting for talking about something together and learning
blanked: The writing ______ had only twelve students, so everyone could share their work.

definition: a feeling or opinion about something

definition: a person who questions whether something is true or real
blanked: The ______ asked to see the proof before believing the magician's claim of real magic.

definition: being together as one and support among members of a group

definition: alone; done without anyone else
blanked: The ______ lighthouse stood alone on the rocky cliff, guiding ships through the fog.

definition: the authority of a country to govern itself on its own
blanked: Every nation's ______ means it has the right to make its own laws without outside control.

definition: happening by nature and without planning
blanked: The ______ applause surprised the singer—he hadn't expected anyone to clap mid-song.

definition: not moving or flowing; not changing or growing
blanked: The ______ pond water turned green with algae because there was no current to keep it fresh.

definition: something that causes a reaction or response

definition: to state exactly what must be done as part of an agreement

definition: the right to vote in elections

blanked: Security cameras provide ______ of the parking lot twenty-four hours a day.

definition: able to continue without depleting resources or causing damage
blanked: Using solar power is ______ because the sun will keep shining for billions of years.

definition: a cost added to goods brought in from another country
blanked: The ______ on foreign steel made imported metal more expensive than locally produced steel.

definition: the science of sorting living things into groups
blanked: In ______, all living things are sorted from broad kingdoms down to individual species.

definition: the period of time someone holds a position
blanked: During her ten-year ______ as principal, she transformed the school into the best in the district.

definition: proof that shows how good or true something is

definition: a mathematical statement that has been proven true

definition: to formally give up a belief or claim

definition: growth or increase by gradual adding
blanked: The ______ of snow on the roof made the family worry about its weight.

definition: to make a formal judgment or decision about a dispute
blanked: The teacher was asked to ______ the argument between the two students.

definition: to make something less pure by adding lower-quality substances
blanked: Adding water to the juice would ______ its natural flavor.

definition: the highest point of growth or the perfect example

definition: formal approval or praise

definition: to take something without having the right to do so

definition: a return to an earlier, more primitive type
blanked: The sudden appearance of spots on the horse was considered an ______.

definition: to divide into two branches or parts

definition: to remove parts of a book or text considered offensive or inappropriate
blanked: The publisher chose to ______ the old story to make it suitable for children.

blanked: Telling someone 'time heals all wounds' is a common ______ that rarely helps.

definition: the rise and fall of the voice when speaking, or a rhythmic pattern
blanked: The poet read with a beautiful ______ that made the words sound like music.

definition: a false story or rumor that is spread to trick people
blanked: The ______ about the school closing turned out to be completely untrue.

definition: giving in or giving way when you cannot win
blanked: The team's ______ to the opposing side ended the long debate.

definition: speed and quickness of movement

definition: to think hard and carefully, using deep focus
blanked: She sat quietly to ______ on the best solution to the difficult problem.

definition: to secretly work together to cheat or to trick someone
blanked: The two students ______ to copy each other's answers during the test.

definition: to mix up two or more things into one by mistake
blanked: It is easy to ______ the words 'affect' and 'effect' because they sound alike.

definition: the state of being related by blood
blanked: The family tree showed the ______ between the cousins.

definition: sharing a border or touching along an edge
blanked: The two ______ states shared a long border along the river.

definition: to cancel or reverse an order or command

definition: a long and angry speech or text attacking something

definition: works well; gets the job done

definition: to criticize someone very harshly

definition: needing immediate action; urgent

definition: to deny or contradict something

definition: the practice of drawing electoral district boundaries to favor one party

definition: impossible to avoid or escape

blanked: The ______ negotiator would not agree to any compromise whatsoever.

definition: harsh criticism or public disgrace

definition: having no purpose or value
